page: Amazon Web Services x2idn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage consists of 2850 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data shows strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in Passmark CPU encryption tests and strong ratings in multi-core CPU benchmarks, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly rated, reaching 326.82 GB/sec for large block reads and 137.6 GB/sec for large block writes. However, single-core performance is average, as reflected in the stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core benchmarks.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. While it excels at memory-intensive workloads and multi-threaded tasks, it exhibits limitations in specific compute areas, such as large-scale LLM text generation where performance is weak. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio, the local NVMe storage, and strong database operation benchmarks, this instance is designed for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching layers, large-scale data processing, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services x2idn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 48 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores, and 1536.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with top-tier performance in encryption and strong results in database operations, Redis SET operations, and memory bandwidth. Conversely, single-core performance is average, and text generation for large language models is weak. This hardware profile, characterized by high memory density and dedicated compute resources, is designed for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise workloads requiring high memory-to-vCPU ratios.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2idn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 1536 GB of DDR4 RAM, 2850 GB of NVMe SSD storage, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized platform featuring high RAM density, dedicated Intel Xeon processors, local NVMe storage, and strong multi-core performance.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 1536 GB of DDR4 RAM providing a high memory-to-core ratio
2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong multi-core CPU, database operations, and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Average single-core performance and weak large-scale LLM text generation
Designed for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
